content.problemStatement:
Chef is a very experienced and well-known cook. He has participated in many cooking competitions in the past — so many that he does not even remember them all.

One of these competitions lasted for a certain number of days. The first day of the competition was day SSS of the week (i.e. Monday, Tuesday etc.) and the last day was day EEE of the week. Chef remembers that the duration of the competition (the number of days between the first and last day, inclusive) was between LLL days and RRR days inclusive. Is it possible to uniquely determine the exact duration of the competition? If so, what is this duration?
constraints: SSS is one of the strings "saturday", "sunday", "monday", "tuesday", "wednesday", "thursday" or "friday"
EEE is one of the strings "saturday", "sunday", "monday", "tuesday", "wednesday", "thursday" or "friday"
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows. The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated strings SSS and EEE, followed by a space and two space-separated integers LLL and RRR.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing: the string "impossible" if there is no duration consistent with all given information, the string "many" if there is more than one possible duration, one integer — the duration of the competition, if its duration is unique

sampleTestCases.input:
3
saturday sunday 2 4
monday wednesday 1 20
saturday sunday 3 5
sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the duration is 2 days, which is within the given range [2, 4]. For the second test case, there are many possible durations, including 3 and 10 days. For the third test case, the duration is 2 days, which is not within the given range [3, 5].

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we first need to calculate the duration of the competition in days. We can do this by finding the difference between the day of the week of the last day and the day of the week of the first day. If the last day is before the first day in the week, we need to add 7 to the difference. Then, we check if the calculated duration is within the given range [LLL, RRR]. If it is, we check if there are other possible durations by adding 7 to the calculated duration and checking if it is also within the given range. If there are no other possible durations, we print the calculated duration. If there are other possible durations, we print "many". If the calculated duration is not within the given range, we print "impossible".
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) — we loop over each test case once
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we use a constant amount of space to store the input and the calculated duration
